episodes.videoTitle: Pope Leo vs. AI, GPT 5.5 Beats Claude, and Sam Altman Walks Back Job Apocalypse | EP #259
episodes.summary: A wide-ranging Moonshots episode that ties current AI advances to social, regulatory, economic, and existential effects.

insights.headline: The Vatican formally reframed AI as a human-dignity issue, not just a safety problem
insights.detail: Pope Leo XIII published a 42,000-word encyclical urging regulation, worker protections, bans on autonomous weapons, and warning about a new Babel syndrome of data-and-profit concentration.

insights.headline: Top-down 90-day pre-release reviews were killed; governments can't regulate AI like old industries
insights.detail: A planned White House executive order requiring pre-release review of models was scuttled after heavy industry pushback.

insights.headline: GPT-5.5 achieved a step-change in coding: coding agents are becoming the workflow
insights.detail: On a new harder benchmark (DeepSuite), GPT-5.5 scored ~70% vs Claude Opus 4.7 at ~54%.

insights.headline: As token prices plunged, usage exploded — Jevons paradox on steroids
insights.detail: Since late 2024 token prices fell roughly 75% (from ~$2/M tokens to $0.50), yet consumption rose to ~25 trillion tokens/month.

insights.headline: The job apocalypse narrative is overstated; disruption is hiring freezes and a solopreneur boom
insights.detail: Current data show concentrated effects (younger workers, hiring freezes) rather than mass unemployment; solo AI founders doubled in a recent quarter.
